items.title: OpenAI launches GPT-5.4 with Pro and Thinking versions | TechCrunch
items.author: techcrunch.com
items.snippet: OpenAI launched GPT-5.4 in early March 2026 as its most capable frontier model for professional and enterprise work. The model unifies capabilities previously split across specialist models — coding, reasoning, and computer use — into a single architecture. It is available in three variants: standard GPT-5.4, GPT-5.4 Thinking (a reasoning-focused version that can show upfront plans mid-response), and GPT-5.4 Pro (optimized for high performance). The API version supports context windows up to 1 m

items.title: OpenAI Newsroom | Company Announcements
items.author: openai.com
items.snippet: OpenAI has been active with multiple announcements in late March 2026. On the product side, ChatGPT received a simplified mobile sidebar and new location sharing controls allowing users to share device or precise location for more relevant local results, rolling out to iOS and web first. ChatGPT Enterprise and EDU also gained a unified Google Drive connector for Docs, Sheets, and Slides management. items.title: How confessions can keep language models honest | OpenAI
items.author: openai.com
items.snippet: OpenAI published several notable research papers and releases in early 2026. In March 2026, key publications include work on chain-of-thought monitorability — studying how reasoning models struggle to control their chains of thought, which OpenAI frames as a safety feature that reinforces monitorability. They also published research on improving instruction hierarchy in frontier LLMs (IH-Challenge), which trains models to prioritize trusted instructions and resist prompt injection attacks. Addit
